subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.1.1: Demonstrate understanding of variety of songs
indicator_description: Listen attentively to songs and sing them with appropriate stress, rhythm and actions
examplars:
1. Identify a variety of familiar songs from learners' background.
2. Lead learners to sing the songs with appropriate stress, rhythm and intonation.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.1.1: Demonstrate understanding of variety of songs
indicator_description: Identify and discuss values in songs
examplars:
1. Select suitable songs for listening, singing/recitation from learners' background. e.g. National Anthem, National Pledge, Ten Galloping Horses Came Through the Town
2. After they have listened to the songs and also sang them, lead them to identify and discuss the values in the songs.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.3.1: Appreciate poems and other pieces of literary materials
indicator_description: Recite poems with stress, rhythm and actions and interpret them in own their words
examplars:
1. Perform a short poem as learners listen and observe.
2. Teach the key words in the poem in context.
3. Have them read it in small groups, pairs and individually.
4. Emphasise correct stress and rhythm.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.3.1: Appreciate poems and other pieces of literary materials
indicator_description: Identify and discuss values in poems
examplars:
1. Have learners recite the poem learnt.
2. Put them in groups to discuss the values in the poem.
3. Have groups share their views with the whole class.
4. Engage learners in recitation for mastery.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.3.1: Appreciate poems and other pieces of literary materials
indicator_description: Compose four-line poems
examplars:
1. Explain the task.
2. Guide learners with examples to choose a suitable topic and contribute suitable lines to it.
3. Write learner's contributions to the topic on the board.
4. Re-arrange sentences in a logical order to form a simple poem.
5. In turns, learners read the poem for enjoyment.
6. Note: Learners should not be made to commit new poems to memory on the same day. (It should be spaced over a number of lessons).

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.4.1: Respond to stories
indicator_description: Retell stories sequentially, including key details
examplars:
1. Tell/read a suitable story to the class e.g. "The Hawk and the Hen".
2. Learners retell the story in detail.
3. Have learners identify the parts of the story as beginning part, middle part and ending part.
4. Through questioning, have learners discuss the various parts of the story i.e. beginning, middle and ending, as in the table below: Title.. What was the event? Where in the story did this event happen? What are the key details of this event? Beginning Middle Ending
5. Put learners into groups to discuss the central message of the story.
6. Have learners retell the story sequentially.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.4.1: Respond to stories
indicator_description: Tell own stories
examplars:
1. Invite learners to tell their own stories to the class.
2. Have the class discuss the elements in the stories told.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.5.1: Perform stories
indicator_description: Role-play a story/play
examplars:
1. Select a suitable story/play learners have heard or read e. g. Ananse and the Black Pot.
2. Have learners identify and discuss the characters and events/key issues in the play/story.
3. Guide learners to role-play the key characters in specific scenes.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.5.2: Talk about key issues in stories/sketches
indicator_description: Identify moral values in sketches and relate them to real life situations
examplars:
1. Have learners discuss the characters and events/key issues in the play.
2. Put learners into groups or pairs depending on the number of characters in the play/drama. Each group should have a leader. Give scenarios (from the story/play) to groups and have members take up roles of the characters. Create dialogues for the sketch and have learners prepare to perform the sketch. Go round and provide support to groups.
3. Have groups take turns to perform their sketch in front of the class.
4. Lead the class with questions to talk about each sketch. They talk about what went well, what needs to be improved, suggest alternative ways of doing it better, the moral values and how they relate to life.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Conversation - Talking about Oneself, Family, People, Customs, Social/Cultural Values and Manners
content_standard: B4.1.6.1: Use culturally acceptable language for communication
indicator_description: Describe/talk about objects, events, dates and time
examplars:
1. Demonstrate the activity by describing a classroom object.
2. Learners give oral descriptions of classroom and other familiar objects such as a table, a school bag, a chair and others, (Learners may ask questions as the description goes on).
3. Festivals: Learners name familiar festivals and talk about them: when they are held, why and how they are celebrated, etc.
4. Birthdays: Learners state their birthdays/dates and write them down using the two formats indicated in the content. (Teacher should check accuracy).
5. Telling the Time: Using a clock face, assist learners to revise telling the time using expressions such as half past 8, quarter past 8, quarter to 8, etc. Assist learners to tell the time throughout the day: 10 minutes past 8; 25 minutes past 9; 20 to 11 etc.
6. Important places in the country: Provide a map of Ghana and assist learners to identify important places and talk about them. Have learners locate these places on the map.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Conversation - Talking about Oneself, Family, People, Customs, Social/Cultural Values and Manners
content_standard: B4.1.6.2: Demonstrate positive listening and viewing attitudes and behaviour by showing attentiveness and understanding
indicator_description: Listen and view attentively and for a sustained period (e.g., look at the person speaking) and maintain eye contact.
examplars:
1. As learners observe and listen attentively, engage in a conversation with one of them on a topic e.g. "Important Places in the District".
2. Repeat procedure in bullet one, above, in talking about names of regions/places, festivals, date, time, familiar objects, etc. Pairs may be invited to perform in front of the class.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Conversation - Talking about Oneself, Family, People, Customs, Social/Cultural Values and Manners
content_standard: B4.1.6.2: Demonstrate positive listening and viewing attitudes and behaviour by showing attentiveness and understanding
indicator_description: Listen and view for the entire duration of a text, a speech, a presentation, a video etc.
examplars:
1. Play a recorded speech by an important personality (e.g. the President) on a relevant topic.
2. Have learners listen to the speech carefully.
3. Each learner prepares a speech in an imagined or assumed position.
4. Working in groups, each learner delivers his/her speech to the group. Groups listen carefully during each delivery.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Conversation - Talking about Oneself, Family, People, Customs, Social/Cultural Values and Manners
content_standard: B4.1.6.3: Use knowledge of language and communicative skills to participate in conversation
indicator_description: Engage in collaborative conversation on topics such as myself, family, personalities etc. with peers
examplars:
1. Model describing yourself: e.g. name, physical features, character, likes and dislikes etc.
2. Guide learners with appropriate questions to give oral descriptions of themselves.
3. Select and describe a family member using relevant vocabulary e.g. sister/brother/parent name, age, how he/she looks like, shape of face, facial marks if any, shape of nose, eyes, etc., height, colour and any minute detail that can be used to identify the person easily.
4. Guide learners with questions and other activities, to mention the name of the Regional Minister for the region in which their school is located.
5. Have learners mention names of personalities in politics such as the Ministers for Education, Health, Agriculture, etc.
6. Through questions and answers have learners talk about the roles and duties of these ministers.
7. Individually, ask learners to talk about their favourite personalities in sports, music, and mention their characteristics, and what they like about the personalities mentioned.
8. Note: Ensure that learners use descriptive words.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Conversation - Talking about Oneself, Family, People, Customs, Social/Cultural Values and Manners
content_standard: B4.1.6.3: Use knowledge of language and communicative skills to participate in conversation
indicator_description: Demonstrate turn taking in conversation on different topics and speak audibly, and express thoughts and feelings clearly
examplars:
1. Demonstrate turn taking with a learner earlier prepared.
2. Learners in pairs, take turns to talk about given topics in groups observing turn taking.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Conversation - Talking about Oneself, Family, People, Customs, Social/Cultural Values and Manners
content_standard: B4.1.6.3: Use knowledge of language and communicative skills to participate in conversation
indicator_description: Ask relevant questions to find out the opinion of others about a given topic
examplars:
1. Demonstrate the activity using a familiar topic.
2. Learners ask and answer questions for clarification about what other learners say on a given topic.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.7.1: Use appropriate skills and strategies to process meaning from texts
indicator_description: Construct meaning from texts based on knowledge of stress, rhythm and intonation
examplars:
1. Use various types of sentences/utterances to model stress and intonation as well as rhythm. You may also read out extracts from stories and poems.
2. Ask learners to sing familiar songs clapping and tapping to the rhythm. Through discussion, introduce learners to stress, rhythm and intonation.
3. In turns, learners use stress, rhythm and intonation that convey meaning in making statements and asking questions.
4. Have learners listen to stories such as "Ananse and the Black Pot", "The Hawk and the Hen", etc. and identify examples of effective use of stress, rhythm and intonation.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.7.1: Use appropriate skills and strategies to process meaning from texts
indicator_description: Make connections with events in narrative texts
examplars:
1. Learners retell the story (e.g. in a chain).
2. Guide learners to identify the events in the story.
3. Put learners in small groups to discuss the events of the story and relate them to one another.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.7.1: Use appropriate skills and strategies to process meaning from texts
indicator_description: Recognise and discuss moral lessons in a story
examplars:
1. Through brainstorming, learners identify, discuss and evaluate moral lessons in narrative texts heard/read.
2. Have them connect to the characters by relating them to real life experiences.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.7.1: Use appropriate skills and strategies to process meaning from texts
indicator_description: Use background knowledge to aid in understanding and building new knowledge while listening to narrative texts
examplars:
1. Have learners listen to a narrative text.
2. Learners in groups identify, discuss and relate the plot of the text to a familiar text.
3. Have learners listen to the text a second time.
4. In groups, learners share what they have learnt in the text.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.7.1: Use appropriate skills and strategies to process meaning from texts
indicator_description: Identify the main idea/gist and details of texts
examplars:
1. Have learners read short stories, newspaper cuttings.
2. Guide them with examples to give the gist and details of the texts.
3. Guide learners to identify the various text contents as they retell and discuss texts read.
4. Assist learners to identify themes from the various texts read or discussed. e. g. love, greed, honesty, hard work, etc.
5. Have them share facts from the story with their peers.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.7.1: Use appropriate skills and strategies to process meaning from texts
indicator_description: Compare and contrast information (two or more ideas) from texts
examplars: 1. Guide learners to compare and contrast the content of different texts read/discussed (pay attention to the differences and the similarities).

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.8.1: Demonstrate understanding in asking and answering questions correctly
indicator_description: Use appropriate pronunciation and intonation in asking and answering questions
examplars:
1. Through role-play, learners ask questions such as: What is your name? How are you? Where do you live?
2. Guide learners to use appropriate pronunciation and intonation in asking and answering questions. e.g. What do you want? Where's your school?
3. Note: A rising intonation for a question and a falling intonation for an answer.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.8.2: Identify and use question tags correctly in speech
indicator_description: Use positive tags, negative tags and auxiliaries in speech
examplars:
1. Discuss and explain what question tags are and give examples.
2. Learners in pairs ask and answer questions.
3. Demonstrate the usage of positive and negative tags.
4. Guide learners in oral practice to use positive and negative tags. e.g. Question: "You have a pen, don't you?" Answer: Yes, I do. Answer: No, I don't.
5. Use the internet to identify the appropriate intonation in asking and answering questions.
6. Through role play, learners demonstrate asking and answering questions.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Giving and Following Commands/ Instructions/Directions and Making and Responding to Requests
content_standard: B4.1.9.1: Use verbs appropriately in commands, requests and directions in speech
indicator_description: Give and respond to commands, instructions and directions
examplars:
1. Give general commands and guide learners to respond appropriately to the commands. e. g. Stand up! Sit down! Carry the bowl! etc.
2. Through role play, group and pair activities, assist learners to give and obey simple commands/instructions. e.g. i. Go to your seat. ii. Bring your exercise book to me. iii. Walk from BI classroom to the Headmaster's office. iv. Turn right, walk twenty steps towards the water tank.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Giving and Following Commands/ Instructions/Directions and Making and Responding to Requests
content_standard: B4.1.9.1: Use verbs appropriately in commands, requests and directions in speech
indicator_description: Make and respond to polite requests for help from peers
examplars:
1. Model making a request to learners.
2. Have learners work in groups to write three tasks they plan to do and need help with, e.g. do homework, carry the table, etc.
3. Learners then go around the class making requests to find classmates who are free to help them with their three tasks, e.g. "Can you please help me do my homework this afternoon?". If a learner is free, they accept the request. But if s/he is not free, they decline the request.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.10.1: Show knowledge of spoken grammar and register
indicator_description: Demonstrate awareness of the features of spoken language (contractions, ellipsis e.g. A: How are you? B: Fine)
examplars: 1. Through role play, demonstrate the features of spoken language. e.g. Contractions: Can't, Shouldn't, etc. Ellipses: A. How are you? B. Fine.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.10.1: Show knowledge of spoken grammar and register
indicator_description: Demonstrate awareness of how meaning is conveyed through appropriate pace, stress, tone - through stories read aloud
examplars:
1. Model reading aloud a level-appropriate story or a paragraph with good pace, stress or intonation.
2. Read the same story or text aloud with poor pace, stress and intonation.
3. Put learners into groups to analyse the types of reading and identify which one conveys meaning and why.
4. Have learners practise reading the story with good pace, stress and intonation.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.10.2: Demonstrate the ability to communicate with accurate pronunciation and appropriate intonation
indicator_description: Speak clearly and fluently, using the appropriate voice qualities (pace, tone etc.)
examplars:
1. Model a speech: choose a familiar topic and make a short speech on it.
2. Guide learners to discuss the speech.
3. Have learners practise in groups to select topics and make speeches on them. Encourage them to speak clearly and fluently using the appropriate voice qualities.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.10.2: Demonstrate the ability to communicate with accurate pronunciation and appropriate intonation
indicator_description: Read aloud clearly, at a good pace and with short expression
examplars: 1. Provide varied texts and guide learners to read them clearly at a good pace and with good expression. The texts can be in the form of poems/rhyme, etc.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.10.3: Plan and present information and ideas for a variety of purposes
indicator_description: Identify the purpose and audience of a speech and set goals in the context of assigned topics (e.g. about familiar persons)
examplars:
1. Using a model speech which must be provided by the teacher, guide learners to identify: i. the purpose of the speech; ii. the audience; iii. the goals set in the context of assigned topics.
2. Note: The speech can be about self, familiar persons, objects and experiences.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.10.3: Plan and present information and ideas for a variety of purposes
indicator_description: Gather and select facts and ideas from one or multiple print and/ or non-print sources, appropriate to the purpose, audience, context and culture
examplars:
1. Guide learners to select interesting topics they want to talk about. Put them into groups and have each group choose a topic.
2. Guide learners to gather ideas from their readers and/or online resources.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.10.3: Plan and present information and ideas for a variety of purposes
indicator_description: Support ideas and points with visual resources to convey meaning appropriate to purpose and context
examplars: 1. Have groups of learners draw pictures to support their ideas or points of view on given topics.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.10.3: Plan and present information and ideas for a variety of purposes
indicator_description: Use effective introductions and conclusions
examplars:
1. Put learners into convenient groups.
2. Guide them to select topics of interest, plan and present speeches to the class.
3. Guide them to introduce and conclude their speeches appropriately.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.10.3: Plan and present information and ideas for a variety of purposes
indicator_description: Elaborate on ideas using explanations
examplars:
1. Explain the need to elaborate on points made. Present examples.
2. Guide learners to use details, concrete examples, to elaborate on their ideas/points of view on given topics.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.1.10.3: Plan and present information and ideas for a variety of purposes
indicator_description: Speak with confidence before peers and maintain eye contact
examplars:
1. Encourage learners doing presentations to speak before different audiences. e. g. small and large groups
2. Invite a learner and model maintaining eye contact while speaking with him or her.
3. Have pairs of learners practise talking to each other on topics of interest while maintaining eye contact.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.2.1: Connect sounds to letters; and blend letters/syllables in order to read and write
indicator_description: Match sounds to their corresponding letter/letter patterns (e.g. initial/final consonants - m, j, f, s, etc., initial short vowels"- a, e, i, o, u", final "y" as vowel, silent letters etc.)
examplars:
1. Introduce the sounds with alphabet songs or rhymes.
2. Guide learners to identify and produce the sounds of given letter patterns. e. g. Teacher: Make the sound as I point to each letter. (Learners begin with the initial/final consonants, initial short vowels, final y as vowel and silent letters).
3. In pairs/groups, learners identify given letter patterns.
4. Have learners apply their knowledge of consonant digraphs.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.2.2: Use reading readiness and word identification skills
indicator_description: Recognise and read words using a variety of cues (e.g. prefixes - word beginning)
examplars:
1. Guide learners to understand that the core of a word is its root and that other words can be got by additions to a root word.
2. Guide learners to form more words using prefixes. e. g. a prefix may create new words. e.g. tell - retell, over - moreover. Note: Prefixes may create antonyms (opposites). e.g. friendly - unfriendly, satisfied - dissatisfied
3. In groups, learners identify several prefixes and use them in sentences. PREFIX(before) ROOT(core) NEW WORD: Mis - Use - misuse; En - Joy - enjoy; Anti - Clockwise - anticlockwise; De - compose - decompose; Poly - clinic - polyclinic. Note: Prefixes have meanings of their own e.g. Ante-before, bi-two, ex-out of, previous, from etc.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Word Families, Rhyming Endings and Common Digraphs
content_standard: B4.2.3.1: Identify rhyming/endings words and common digraphs
indicator_description: Use common rhyming/ending words to decode words. e. g. at, pat, mat, fat, etc.
examplars:
1. Revise the lesson on prefixes. Let learners know that just as we have family names (surnames), there are words that have the same ending that belong to the family of rhyming words.
2. Through brainstorming have learners come up with words that belong to a particular rhyming family. Have children identify these rhyming words as they read.
3. Have learners hunt for these words and build a portfolio on them.
4. Guide learners to build on words. e.g. -at= cat- bat - hat; -all = call-fall - wall; -it = sit-pit- hit; -ot = tot - pot - cot

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Word Families, Rhyming Endings and Common Digraphs
content_standard: B4.2.3.1: Identify rhyming/endings words and common digraphs
indicator_description: Read words with digraphs to make meaningful sentences
examplars:
1. Revise the lesson on prefixes.
2. Through brainstorming have learners come up with as many words that belong to a particular digraph. Have learners identify these digraphs as they read.
3. Have learners hunt for these words and build a portfolio on them.
4. Ask learners to identify digraphs in simple sentences. e. g. The girls shouted. (sh as in shout). Note: Common digraphs include ch, ck, gh, gr, sch, sc
5. Learners form their own sentences using words with digraphs.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.4.1: Identify and use diphthongs to decode words
indicator_description: Use closing diphthongs e.g. /ei/, /ai/, /oi/ to make meaningful sentences
examplars:
1. Teach one diphthong at a time.
2. Write words with each of the diphthongs on the board.
3. Pronounce the words and have learners repeat after you.
4. Learners come up with examples of words that have the diphthongs.
5. Have learners identify these diphthongs as they read the words. E.g. ei - take, oi - boy, ai- why, try.
6. Learners form their own sentences using words having diphthongs.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.5.1: Identify and use consonant blends and clusters in reading
indicator_description: Orally produce single-syllable words by blending sounds (phonemes), including consonant blends
examplars:
1. Guide learners to play games like "Tapping Out" to bring out the single-syllable words.
2. Demonstrate blending by sounding out letters separately and blending/bringing them together into syllables and words. e.g. p-l-o-t = plot; b-l-a-c-k = black; g-r-o-w = grow.
3. Have learners identify words containing particular blends e.g. bl, br, cr, cl, gr, gl.
4. Tapping Out Game: "Tapping Out" is about using the fingers to teach decoding /blending the sound of letters. Each finger represents a letter sound of single-syllable words. Turn your back to the class and raise your right hand. Raise the index finger straight as learners watch. Bring the index finger down to meet the thumb as you make the sound. Use fingers to demonstrate blending.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.5.1: Identify and use consonant blends and clusters in reading
indicator_description: Use the spelling-sound correspondences for common consonant digraphs
examplars:
1. Through discussion, have learners come up with many words that have a particular digraph.
2. Have learners decode these digraphs as they read.
3. Assist learners to get the spelling-sounds correspondence right.
4. Play a game of the spelling-sound correspondence of selected digraphs. In groups, have learners sound out words and identify the digraphs in them. The group that gets a lot of this right carries the day. e.g. sh: shoe, show, shirt, shine, shell, shame, shake, shop, bush, wash, brush, marsh, crush, fish

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.6.1: Understand word meanings and usages
indicator_description: Use level-appropriate content words (nouns, verbs, adjectives and adverbs) and function words (prepositions) appropriately in spoken and written communication
examplars:
1. Using examples, have learners see the difference between content and function words.
2. Provide a substitution table of content words and function words for learners to construct sentences from. Help learners to sort out the different content words and function words in groups.
3. Assist learners to understand that these words combine into sentences to help us make proper meaning of them.
4. Let learners make sentences using function and content words.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.6.1: Understand word meanings and usages
indicator_description: Use the following terms: compound word, idiom, simile, synonym, antonym, pre-fix, suffix, phrasal verb etc. in spoken and written expressions
examplars:
1. Introduce the terms one at a time with several examples.
2. Elicit examples from learners.
3. Learners identify examples from different sources, e.g. their readers, story books, newspapers (Junior Graphic) and use them in sentences of their own.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.6.2: Build vocabulary
indicator_description: Develop a rich vocabulary stock through extensive reading of age-appropriate texts
examplars:
1. Introduce learners to a variety of story books and vocabulary portfolios and stress the need to build and use the new vocabulary in sentences.
2. Display some of the vocabulary in class, on your word trees and in sentences. Have learners work in pairs, individually and in groups, to do same.
3. Guide learners to make meaning from the context in which words have been used.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.6.3: Demonstrate a rich vocabulary that supports the development of listening, reading, speaking, writing and presentation skills
indicator_description: Deduce meaning of words from how they are used in context e.g. near synonyms: cool - cold - freezing), homonyms: flour/flower
examplars:
1. Using several examples guide learners to make meaning from the context in which words have been used.(contextual meaning)
2. Learners work in pairs and in groups to work out the meaning of given words from their context.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.6.3: Demonstrate a rich vocabulary that supports the development of listening, reading, speaking, writing and presentation skills
indicator_description: Deduce meaning of words from how they relate to one another (synonyms, antonyms)
examplars:
1. Reverse the meaning of the terms using examples.
2. Have learners play games (e.g. Synonym Tree Game) to reinforce their knowledge of antonyms and synonyms.
3. Ask learners to find synonyms and antonyms of words made from context to build on their vocabulary.
4. Learners may match words with their synonyms/antonyms.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.6.4: Use words appropriately for purpose, audience, context and culture
indicator_description: Expand vocabulary stock through affixation
examplars:
1. Revise prefixes and suffixes by breaking down some common words into their roots- prefix/suffix components. e.g. happy + ness = happiness; un + happy + ness = unhappiness
2. Guide learners to play games, e.g. Affixes Game. Write prefixes and suffixes on cards. Learners take turns to pick these from the basket or bag. If a learner is able to add a root to the prefix or the suffix and read out the word correctly, he/she keeps it. At the end of the game, the child with the largest number of cards wins the game.
3. Learners may also work in groups to derive words using given prefixes and suffixes and using them in sentences.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.6.4: Use words appropriately for purpose, audience, context and culture
indicator_description: Recognise the playful use of words in spoken and written language (jokes, riddles)
examplars: 1. In pairs/ groups, have learners play on words in educative jokes, riddles and puns. e.g. Riddle, Riddle: I am something that came into this world with earrings. Who am I? Answer: Coal pot

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.6.4: Use words appropriately for purpose, audience, context and culture
indicator_description: Use words suitable for purpose, audience, context and culture in relation to type of texts (exposition/explanation)
examplars:
1. Explain to children the key elements in all writing e.g. purpose, audience etc. These help to make the understanding of the text easy. That is, they play an integral part in the process. Guide learners to understand each key element.
2. Introduce learners to a variety of texts and registers which may be used in given situations.
3. Have learners work in groups to write short texts on familiar topics.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.7.1: Process and comprehend level-appropriate texts
indicator_description: Construct meaning from texts read
examplars:
1. Select level-appropriate texts for learners.
2. Take learners through Before Reading activities such as picture walk, prediction and Before Reading questions etc. to elicit their prior knowledge.
3. Have learners use their previous knowledge/experience, contextual clues and While Reading questions to make meaning from the text as they read.
4. Provide After Reading questions to help learners make meaning from the text read.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.7.1: Process and comprehend level-appropriate texts
indicator_description: Note and recall main ideas in a sequence
examplars:
1. Using the SQ3R strategy, learners recall main ideas. Learners:
2. S-Survey the text- (learners skim, by going through the chapter, noting heading/sub-heading and other understanding features.
3. Q- Question - Generate questions about the content of the text.
4. 3R: Read - Read for more information. Recite - Retrieve information from meaning. Review - Go over the main ideas in the text using your own words.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.7.1: Process and comprehend level-appropriate texts
indicator_description: Skim for main ideas in texts
examplars: 1. Guide learners to scan/skim the text either for general or specific information as a preparation for detailed reading.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.7.1: Process and comprehend level-appropriate texts
indicator_description: Read level-appropriate texts silently and closely for comprehension.
examplars:
1. Briefly discuss the need for silent reading.
2. Guide learners with pre-reading questions.
3. Assign them to read the text silently to find answers to the pre-reading questions.
4. Note: Depending on the length of the text, it may be read in bits.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.7.2: Apply critical reading, implied meaning, higher order thinking, judgment and evaluation
indicator_description: Respond to a text with reasons
examplars:
1. Identify and present points of view in a text.
2. Guide learners with questions to discuss the text and answer a variety of questions. They should state reasons for their points/ point of view.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.7.2: Apply critical reading, implied meaning, higher order thinking, judgment and evaluation
indicator_description: Make connections between a text and personal/real life experiences
examplars:
1. Guide learners with questions e.g. applicative questions to connect the reading text with background knowledge.
2. Encourage learners to come up with their personal opinions and viewpoints.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.7.2: Apply critical reading, implied meaning, higher order thinking, judgment and evaluation
indicator_description: Demonstrate awareness of the structure of texts (e.g. introduction, body, conclusion)
examplars:
1. Guide learners to identify the parts of a text e.g. Introduction, Body and Conclusion.
2. They should summarise each part and after that, make a full summary of the text.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.7.3: Demonstrate an understanding of the use of words and phrases as used in a text
indicator_description: Determine the contextual meaning of words and phrases
examplars:
1. Phrases have their meaning different from the individual words.
2. Guide learners to make the right meaning using the text to get the contextual meaning.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.8.1: Construct meaning from texts read
indicator_description: Read silently and reasonably for meaning from level-appropriate texts
examplars: 1. Learners read silently making reference to pictures, illustrations, etc. to make meaning of texts. Encourage learners to do independent work. Note: You may guide learners with questions to do this.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.8.1: Construct meaning from texts read
indicator_description: Find meaning of words as used in context
examplars: 1. Guide pupils to learn new words through the procedure below: -Write key words on the board, one at a time. -Have learners attempt to pronounce the word. -Model the pronunciation for learners to repeat. -From the passage, ask learners to write the sentence in which the new word is and read it out. -Use Think-Pair-Share strategy to have learners find meaning in context. -Have them think of other words that can replace these words. -Ask pupils to use the words in sentences. Note: You can vary the procedure in teaching new words.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.8.1: Construct meaning from texts read
indicator_description: Answer questions based on the passage read
examplars:
1. Precede questions with silent reading of the text.
2. In asking questions, follow the steps below: Pose the question. Give learners time to reflect. Call a learner to answer the question.
3. Learners give accurate and specific answers to questions. (You could do it orally sometimes). They may read out portions of the text where the answers are found.
4. Ask different types of levels of questions.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.9.1: Read fluently to enhance comprehension
indicator_description: Read grade- level texts at good pace, with accuracy and expression
examplars:
1. Take learners through pre-reading, while-reading and after-reading activities.
2. In cases where text books are not available, teacher should use carefully selected grade-level appropriate texts.
3. As learners listen and follow in their books, read a paragraph or two aloud at a good pace, with accuracy and expression.
4. Have individuals take turns to practise reading aloud at a good pace, with accuracy and expression.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.9.1: Read fluently to enhance comprehension
indicator_description: Use recognition strategies to confirm understanding of level-appropriate texts
examplars:
1. As learners read the grade-level texts, attention should be given to the words, sentences and paragraphs.
2. Use re-reading, vocabulary and right intonation to self-correct.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.10.1: Read and summarise passages read
indicator_description: Summarise level-appropriate passages/texts orally
examplars:
1. Learners read and re-read a text to identify the main idea in a given paragraph.
2. Guide learners with examples to restate information read in a few words.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.2.10.1: Read and summarise passages read
indicator_description: Write short summary of a level-appropriate passage/text read
examplars:
1. Guide learners to write a summary of the passages taking note of the main ideas.
2. In pairs or groups, learners summarise specific paragraphs of a passage to be presented to the class.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4.3.1.1: Apply knowledge of different types of nouns in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use nouns to identify people, animals, events and objects
examplars:
1. Have learners read simple sentences having names of people, animals, events and objects from the board.
2. Put learners in groups to identify the names of people, animals, events and objects as nouns.
3. Have learners form sentences with names of people, animals, events and objects and underline these nouns in the sentences.
4. Have them note that common nouns start with small letters.
5. Have learners identify common nouns from paragraphs/passages.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4.3.1.1: Apply knowledge of different types of nouns in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use: proper nouns - refer to cities and countries and), Common nouns
examplars:
1. Proper Nouns: Have learners read simple sentences having names of cities and countries from the board.
2. Put learners into groups to identify the names of cities and countries on a globe or map.
3. Learners form sentences with names of other cities and countries.
4. Have learners identify proper nouns from paragraphs/passages.
5. Common Nouns: Introduce common nouns in context. Have learners read simple sentences containing common nouns on the board.
6. Put learners into groups to identify the common nouns.
7. Have learners form sentences using the common nouns identified.
8. Have learners identify common nouns from paragraphs/passages.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4.3.1.1: Apply knowledge of different types of nouns in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use collective nouns to refer to a group of objects and people
examplars:
1. Have learners read sentences containing collective nouns e.g. team of players, a class of, a swarm of bees, a troop of monkeys, a flock of sheep, a bunch of keys.
2. Explain to learners that collective nouns behave like singular nouns. Therefore, they are used with singular verbs.
3. Provide the collective nouns and their groups for learners to identify. Let them form sentences using the collective nouns. e.g. i. A bunch of keys is on the table. ii. A team of players is running around the field.
4. Have learners form sentences with collective nouns.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4.3.1.1: Apply knowledge of different types of nouns in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use abstract nouns to refer to concepts and ideas.
examplars:
1. Write sentences containing abstract nouns on the board.
2. Have learners take turns to read them and identify the nouns.
3. Explain abstract nouns as names of things that cannot be seen, felt, nor touched e. g. air, beauty, peace, etc.
4. Have learners give examples of abstract nouns, use them in sentences and underline them.
5. Learners identify concrete and abstract nouns from short paragraphs/passages.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4. 3.2.1: Apply knowledge of different types of determiners in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use the definite and indefinite articles "a" and "an" to refer to a person, animal, event, time or objects in general
examplars:
1. Have learners read sentences containing articles. Indefinite articles: e.g. I bought an orange. a car, a sheep, a tree, an apple, an orange, an egg, etc. Definite article: I put the book on the table.
2. Use several examples to explain the use of the definite and indefinite articles.
3. Nouns such as music, soup, soap, do not require any determiners. Thus show further that it is possible to say: i. I love music. ii. I enjoy soup.
4. Put learners into small groups to form sentences with a, an, the.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4. 3.2.1: Apply knowledge of different types of determiners in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use quantifiers to show quantities
examplars:
1. Have learners read sentences with quantifiers from the board. e. g. some, few/little, one, two, No, all /every etc.
2. Put some pictures on the wall.
3. Use more examples to show that these quantifiers are used with non-countable nouns: few, little, etc. e. g. little water, little bread, few books, etc.
4. Have learners write sentences using quantifiers e.g. many books, a piece of chalk and identify them from given sentences/texts.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4. 3.2.1: Apply knowledge of different types of determiners in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use possessive pronouns to show possession
examplars:
1. Write sentences with possessive pronouns on the board and have learners read them. e.g. i. I gave my pen to John. ii. The women went to their rooms.
2. Have learners form oral sentences using possessive pronouns.
3. Put them into groups to use possessive pronouns to write sentences and underline the possessive pronouns.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4. 3.2.1: Apply knowledge of different types of determiners in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use demonstratives: this/that, these/those
examplars:
1. Write sentences with demonstratives. i. This boy is from Cape Coast.
2. Have learners read the sentences aloud.
3. Explain to learners the use of the demonstratives. e.g. "this" and "that" are used before singular nouns e.g. this girl, that girl.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4. 3.2.1: Apply knowledge of different types of determiners in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use interrogative determiners "which, whose"- to find out about specific persons or objects
examplars:
1. Present several examples in context and explain their use.
2. Have learners identify them in sentences and short texts.
3. Let them use the interrogatives in their own sentences. e. g. Which house is bigger?

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4.3.3.1: Apply knowledge of different types of pronouns in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use different types of pronouns: "Personal" - to identify people, activities and objects; "Interrogative" - "who, what" to find out a person's identity, specific information about a person, time, objects or events
examplars:
1. i. Personal pronouns - I, You, He/She, We, etc. Write sentences with personal pronouns. e. g. You are late. Provide learners with a variety of practice activities.
2. ii. Interrogative pronouns - Who, What, Where, etc. Introduce interrogative pronouns with several examples in context.
3. Provide a passage and have learners identify interrogative pronouns.
4. Let learners use interrogative pronouns in sentences.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4.3.4.1: Apply the knowledge of adjectives in communication
indicator_description: 1. Use adjectives to make comparison e. g: fast/slow (Ama is fast but Kofi is slow.) good/bad, fast/faster, slow/slower
examplars:
1. Revise adjectives. Have learners identify adjectives in sentences and use them in sentences of their own.
2. Introduce regular adjective forms: Regular adjectives form their imperative by adding er. e.g. small-smaller, big-bigger, etc.
3. Assist learners with examples to use comparative adjectives appropriately. e.g. i. Ama is taller than Kwesi. ii. This ruler is longer than that one.
4. Learners form sentences with the comparative forms of adjectives.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4.3.5.1: Apply the knowledge of verbs in communication
indicator_description: Use different types of verbs: Main verb, Helping verb (primary auxiliary and modal auxiliary)
examplars:
1. Main verbs: Have learners revise verbs by reading sentences from the board and identifying the verbs. Have learners write sentences with given verbs.
2. Auxiliary verbs: Let learners read sentences with auxiliary verbs. e.g. i. The boy is going to school. ii. The girls are reading. Note: An auxiliary verb is also known as a helping verb e.g. is, are, am, was, were.
3. Have learners use auxiliary verbs in sentences and underline them.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4.3.5.1: Apply the knowledge of verbs in communication
indicator_description: Use appropriate subject-verb agreement Singular/plural
examplars:
1. Revise nouns and verbs with simple examples.
2. Through discussion and examples help learners to realise rules that govern sentence formation: A Singular subject goes with singular verb. e. g. The boy is in the room. A Plural subject goes with a plural verb. e. g. The girls are happy. Collective nouns take singular verb. e. g. The team is playing tomorrow.
3. Provide sentences for groups to identify the subjects and verbs.
4. Have learners write their own sentences to be presented to the class.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4.3.5.1: Apply the knowledge of verbs in communication
indicator_description: Use the simple present form of verbs in sentences: For habitual actions, For timeless and universal statements, For facts which may change or hold true indefinitely, For instantaneous present, For scheduled future actions
examplars:
1. Using several examples demonstrate the use of the simple present form of verbs in the sentences (for habitual actions/ for timeless and universal statements):
2. Habitual actions. e.g. i. Esi reads every day. ii. Joe sweeps every morning.
3. Create situations for learners to use verbs to express habitual actions. e.g. Tell the class what you do every morning.
4. Timeless and universal statements. e.g. i. The sun rises in the east and sets in the west. ii. Ghana is in Africa.
5. Provide ample practice using language drills.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4.3.5.1: Apply the knowledge of verbs in communication
indicator_description: Use regular form of the simple past tense of verbs Regular e.g. play-played
examplars:
1. Revise main and auxiliary verbs by having learners identify them in sentences.
2. Introduce learners to the concept of regular verbs: Regular verbs form their past tense by adding "d" or "ed" e.g. play played, walk walked, bake= baked.
3. Elicit examples from learners and have them used sentences.
4. The irregular verbs form their past tense differently. They do not add "d" or "ed".
5. Provide a passage having regular and irregular verbs in the present tense.
6. Learners rewrite the sentences in the past.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4.3.5.1: Apply the knowledge of verbs in communication
indicator_description: Use the simple past form of verbs for: Completed actions or events, Regular actions in the past
examplars:
1. Revise the simple present tense by having learners say what they do regularly e.g. every morning.
2. Learners listen to a simple story in which several completed actions have occurred.
3. Discuss the story and have learners identify the verbs.
4. Learners engage in a conversion drill for practice.
5. Learners write simple sentences in the past tense using (both regular and irregular verbs).

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4.3.5.1: Apply the knowledge of verbs in communication
indicator_description: Use the imperative form of the verb to give commands or orders, make suggestions
examplars:
1. Use situations to demonstrate commands.
2. Provide situations for learners to give and respond to commands in parts or groups.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4.3.6.1: Apply the knowledge of different types of adverbs in communication
indicator_description: Use adverbs of time and place appropriately
examplars:
1. Revise adverbs by having learners identify them in sentences.
2. Present adverbs of place in context.
3. Provide practice in identifying and using adverbs. For practice use questions and answers, and illustrations.
4. Drill learners to practise the appropriate use of adverbs of place. e.g. i. Where is the cat? The cat is here. ii. Where is Kojo? Kojo is outside.
5. Examples of adverbs of place are: here, there, outside, inside, etc.
6. Ask learners to use adverbs of place to form correct sentences.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4.3.6.1: Apply the knowledge of different types of adverbs in communication
indicator_description: Use adverbs of time to modify verbs.
examplars:
1. Present adverbs of place in context.
2. Examples of adverb of time are yesterday, today, tomorrow, etc.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4.3.7.1: Understand and use idiomatic expressions appropriately in speech and in writing
indicator_description: Use idiomatic expressions appropriately in communication
examplars:
1. Present examples of idiomatic expressions in context.
2. Discuss each with learners to bring out it's meaning.
3. Have learners use the expressions they have learnt in sentences guided with further examples.
4. Put learners into groups to determine the meaning of given idiomatic expressions in context.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4.3.8.1: Apply the knowledge of conjunctions in speech and in writing
indicator_description: Identify and use simple conjunctions - and, but, or, nor to link: similar ideas, contrasting ideas, show choices/express alternatives
examplars:
1. Introduce the conjunctions one at a time in context.
2. Elicit examples of sentences with conjunctions from learners.
3. Put on cards simple sentences. e.g. i. Esi is tall. Ama is short. ii. Esi is tall but Ama is short.
4. Use Combination Drill for learners to practise the use of conjunctions e.g. i. Learner A: I bought a pen... Learner B: I bought a pen and a notebook. ii. Learner C: I will eat fufu...... iii. Learner D: I will eat fufu or kenkey.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4.3.9.1: Apply the knowledge of modals in speech and in writing
indicator_description: Use modals to express a variety of meanings: can: conveys ability, may: asks for permission, expresses politeness, possibility, must: obligation or compulsion, necessity, shall / will: prediction, intention, determination etc., could: tentativeness, politeness, would: politeness, might: possibility, should: obligation, used to: for past activities or events, have to, ought to and need to: for obligation
examplars:
1. Introduce the modals one at a time.
2. Have learners read sentences containing modals.
3. Learners use modals to form sentences and choose the right modals to fill the gaps.
4. Use drills to give practice.
5. Note: Modals come before the main verbs in sentences.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B4.3.10.1: Apply the knowledge of prepositions in oral and written communication
indicator_description: Use prepositions to convey a variety of meanings: Direction e.g. along, Period of Time, Purpose, Possession, Comparison e.g. taller than
examplars:
1. Have learners describe the positions of classroom objects using such sentences as: i. The clock is on the wall. ii. The cupboard is in the corner. iii. The waste paper basket is under the table.
2. Briefly explain the functions of prepositions.
3. Introduce a Question and Answer drill to give learners practice. Learner A: Where is your pen? Learner B: It is on the table.
4. Write some of learners' answers on the chalkboard and guide them to identify the prepositions.
5. Note: Ensure that the following prepositions are used: Direction = towards the mountain; Time = at six o'clock; Purpose = for, in order to; Possession = for, to; Comparison = than; Support or opposition = for you, against me.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.4.2.1: Copy and rewrite sentences correctly
indicator_description: Write clearly using joined letters of consistent size
examplars:
1. Stage 1: Write letters with a flick e.g. a, t, m, n, w, d
2. Stage 2: Join pairs of letters. e.g. at, am, et, de
3. Stage 3: Join the letters of a word. e.g. and, kettle, can, catch
4. Stage 4: Copy sentences and passages
5. Procedure: Demonstrate the exercise, Give practice, Give exercise, Provide feedback

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.4.2.1: Copy and rewrite sentences correctly
indicator_description: Use simple sentences clearly and correctly
examplars:
1. Begin proper nouns with capital letters in sentences. e.g. i. Accra is the capital of Ghana. ii. Esi went to Mfantsiman School.
2. Have learners read out sentences and identify proper nouns.
3. Let learners note the use of capital letters.
4. Have them copy the sentences into their exercise books.
5. Present and give practice on the named marks of punctuation marks similarly: e.g. i. She is a teacher. ii. How are you? iii. I bought a pencil, ruler and a book.
6. Discuss the importance of these punctuation marks. i. Full stop (.) marks the end of a sentence. ii. Question mark (?) is used to ask questions. iii. Comma (,) is used when listing items.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.4.6.1: Develop, organise and express ideas cohesively in writing for a variety of purposes, audience, and contexts
indicator_description: Choose appropriate ways and modes of writing for a variety of purposes, audiences, and contexts, and organise facts, ideas and/or points of view in a way appropriate to the mode of delivery, using appropriate text features
examplars:
1. Select sample essay types/texts showing a variety of modes of writing from learners' readers and/teacher's resource. e.g. i. Expository Writing - how something works. ii. Narrative - how something happened. iii. Description - how something/someone appears. iv. Argument - how an opinion can be stated and supported.
2. Put learners into groups of five. For each mode of writing, give a sample text to each group to study and, through appropriate questions, guide them to identify the following: i. the main idea(s); ii. the mode of writing; iii. the purpose; iv. the audience.
3. Note: Present one mode of writing at a time. For each mode of writing, select a paragraph, jumble the sentences and have learners rearrange them into a coherent piece. (Rearrangement of jumbled sentences). You may also use other controlled composition strategies such as matching, completion and blank filling.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.4.6.1: Develop, organise and express ideas cohesively in writing for a variety of purposes, audience, and contexts
indicator_description: Identify the main idea and minor ideas/supporting details in a paragraph
examplars:
1. Provide sample paragraphs that have main ideas and supporting details.
2. Discuss one of these and guide learners to identify the main and supporting ideas.
3. Have learners work in groups of four to read and identify the main idea and supporting ideas. Guide them with the questions: i. What is the paragraph talking about/What is the main idea in the paragraph? ii. Which sentence has the main idea? iii. Which sentences say something about the main idea?

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4. 4.9.1: Apply the skills and strategies for idea generation, selection, development, organisation and revision in writing
indicator_description: Select a topic of choice on issues in the immediate environment, brainstorm and organise ideas before writing
examplars:
1. Take learners through the writing process:
2. Prewriting stage: Put learners in groups of four (4). Each member suggests a topic. Each group discusses it's topics and selects one. Each team presents their topic to the class and writes it on the board.
3. Have groups plan their composition by identifying and discussing the purpose, audience and the context of the piece.
4. Invite groups to present their work and have the whole class give feedback.
5. Use learner strategies such as brainstorming to help learners generate ideas appropriate to the topic.
6. Have learners use graphic organisers to organise the ideas generated. e. g. Brainstorming. Brainstorming involves expressing one's own views and ideas to contribute to resolve a problem. The activity can be done as a whole class, in small groups or in pairs. The high-ability learners may take leading roles. Note: Do not evaluate learners' ideas but accept and record each idea on the board. Assure them that they are not required to justify or explain the ideas they generate.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.4.9.2: Develop and express ideas coherently and cohesively in writing
indicator_description: Develop ideas into a one-paragraph draft using appropriate nouns or pronouns within and across sentences to aid cohesion and avoid ambiguity
examplars:
1. Writing stage: Have learners work independently to write a first draft. Advise learners to: i. Concentrate on getting ideas on paper, not to produce a perfect piece of writing. ii. Use the ideas generated but feel free to add new ideas that occur to them as they write.
2. Be sure their writing has a beginning, middle and an ending.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.4.9.3: Apply strategies for improving drafts for publishing
indicator_description: Review and revise the one-paragraph draft taking out irrelevant details
examplars:
1. Have pupils revise their first draft. Ask them to set aside their first draft for a while and return to it to read over slowly and critically, and reread it several times.
2. Have learners use these questions as a revision guide: i. Is my topic interesting? ii. Does the beginning catch the reader's attention? iii. Are the ideas easy to understand? iv. Do all the sentences say something about the topic?
3. Encourage learners to make as many changes as necessary to improve their work.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.4.9.3: Apply strategies for improving drafts for publishing
indicator_description: Proofread draft, checking capitalisation, usage, punctuation and spelling
examplars:
1. Have learners proofread the revised draft of their writing with focus on the conventions of punctuation, capitalisation, spellings, etc.
2. Let them do peer editing. Learners check their partners' writing for errors (punctuations, spelling, etc.)
3. Have learners write a neat final copy and read it once again to check for errors.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.4.9.3: Apply strategies for improving drafts for publishing
indicator_description: Display writing piece for other peers to read
examplars:
1. Have learners publish their work by preparing and showing it to others in small groups or in pairs. Allow them to post their work on the notice board or show it to all other learners in the class one after the other.
2. Encourage learners to share their work with their family members.
3. Have them keep their work in their portfolios. They may type their work and publish it in the class magazine.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.4.10.1: Narrate situations, express feelings and convey point of view about the world/or fictional world
indicator_description: Write about real or imagined experiences or events following story structure (beginning, middle and ending), using appropriate nouns or pronouns within and across sentences to aid cohesion
examplars:
1. Describing the setting and developing the plot (events in the story).
2. Describing characters with elaboration.
3. Using first and third persons.
4. Guide learners to narrate an event they participated in e.g. my first day at the national theatre. Learners tell the story sequentially: beginning, middle and end. (Learners are grouped to write their own story based on a chosen experience agreed on by all. Let them build a mind map to guide their writing.)
5. Let learners use adjectives and adverbs in describing the physical appearance and state of their characters.
6. Guide them with examples to write from the first person point of view to start with and later change the same story to read from the third person point of view.
7. Learners edit their work first in their groups, then across groups. Let learners then publish their stories on the class notice board so each group can read another's story.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.4.11.1: Create texts
indicator_description: Write freely about topics of choice in their immediate environment
examplars:
1. Have learners, in pairs, think-pair-share and choose a topic from their immediate environment they will want to write about.
2. Guide learners to brainstorm and generate ideas.
3. Have learners organise their ideas and write their first draft.
4. They then peer edit their work.
5. Have them present their work for class discussion and correction.
6. They then write the final draft and display their work for their peers to read.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.4.11.1: Create texts
indicator_description: Write poems and imaginative, narrative stories and illustrate them
examplars:
1. Guide learners to read a poem and let them write a parallel one to start as group presentations.
2. Learners then choose topics and write a four- or five-line poem and illustrate it.
3. Guide them to edit each other's work by exchanging with other groups.
4. Have groups present their work to the class.
5. Encourage reactions from the class.
6. Imaginative narrative: Guide learners through relevant questions to write imaginative stories on topics/themes of their choice.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.4.12.1: Demonstrate knowledge of descriptive words/expressions in writing
indicator_description: Use descriptive words/expressions to describe places, personal experiences and events
examplars:
1. Note: The teacher should consider level-appropriate vocabulary and grammar.
2. Revise adjectives by having learners describe familiar people, objects and places in their environment.
3. Learners in their groups talk about interesting places they have visited.
4. Guide them to describe places of their choice using knowledge of adjectives.
5. Have them do peer editing and share their work with the class.
6. They repeat the procedure above to describe events, situations and personal experiences.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.4.13.1: Support an opinion in writing
indicator_description: Support opinions with simple sentences
examplars:
1. Present a motion to learners. e. g. A doctor is more important than a farmer.
2. Discuss the meaning and implication of the motion with pupils and invite them to take a stand either for or against the motion.
3. Put learners into two groups: for the motion and against the motion and write down what they think about it.
4. Groups read out their opinions to the class for discussions.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.4.13.2: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence
indicator_description: Introduce claim(s) and support them with clear reasons and relevant evidence
examplars:
1. Working in groups (as in B4.7.1.1), learners provide reasons for the opinions they hold on a topic.
2. Guide groups to provide evidence for the opinions they hold.
3. Have groups present their work for discussions.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.4.13.2: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence
indicator_description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to clarify the relationships between claim(s) and reasons
examplars:
1. Lead learners to discuss appropriate language for arguments e. g. use of adjectives in their various forms.
2. You may read out model argumentative essays to guide learners as they listen carefully to identify useful phrases, clauses and expressions.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.4.13.2: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence
indicator_description: Establish and maintain a formal style
examplars:
1. Using models, discuss the basic structure of an argumentative piece: Introduction; Reasons for the stand taken; Conclusion.
2. Have groups present full compositions using this structure for class discussions.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.4.13.2: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence
indicator_description: Provide a concluding statement that follows from argument presented
examplars:
1. Have learners listen to a debate on a familiar topic.
2. Learners in groups discuss the debate.
3. Teach the features of a debate.
4. Guide learners to select a debatable topic. Divide the class into two to go through the writing process to prepare to debate the topic.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.4.14.1: Write informative essays
indicator_description: Write words giving information about family
examplars:
1. Guide learners with a model to talk about themselves. This is a whole class discussion with individual learners saying things about themselves.
2. Let learners write about themselves. In pairs learners edit each other's work and identify words and sentences that give information about the partner which they did not know previously.
3. In groups learners research about their school and write a short history about it.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.4.14.2: Write an event of the day
indicator_description: Write picture events about personal experiences and make radio/TV presentations
examplars:
1. Let learners watch TV news at home and write their own news from the pictures they see.
2. Put learners into groups. Let them brainstorm and write activities of the day.
3. Let each group choose an interesting event and write the news to broadcast to the class.
4. Let each group choose one person to present the news.
5. Help learners to organise a radio or TV talk show.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.4.15.1: Write informal letters on given topics
indicator_description: Write to friends about personal experiences using appropriate letter formats
examplars:
1. Present samples of friendly letters to learners.
2. Let learners read samples in groups and identify important features of friendly letters.
3. Discuss these features with learners.
4. Each group writes a friendly letter (about their personal experiences) showing the important features.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.5.2.1: Show understanding of how punctuations are used appropriately in writing
indicator_description: Use the comma: before and after "Yes" and "No" in sentences, after addressing a person, e.g. Kofi, can you help me?
examplars:
1. Provide learners with sample texts for them to identify the target punctuation marks. e.g. Yes, please. No, thank you. Kofi, can you help me?
2. Give them practice in using the punctuation marks. They may copy and punctuate unpunctuated sentences and short paragraphs.
3. Have them write sentences to demonstrate understanding of the use of the comma before and after "Yes" and "No" in sentences; after addressing a person.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.5.3.1: Apply knowledge of different types of nouns in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use nouns in sentences to identify people, animals, events and objects
examplars:
1. Have learners read simple sentences with names of people, animals, events and objects on the board.
2. Put learners in groups to identify the names of people, animals, events and objects as nouns.
3. Have learners form sentences with names of people, animals, events and objects and underline these names/nouns in the sentences.
4. Have them note that common nouns start with small letters.
5. Have learners identify common nouns from paragraphs/passages.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.5.3.1: Apply knowledge of different types of nouns in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use: Proper nouns - refer to cities and countries, Common nouns
examplars:
1. Proper nouns: Have learners read simple sentences with names of cities and countries from the board.
2. Put learners into groups to identify the names of cities and countries.
3. Have learners identify proper nouns from paragraphs/passages. Have learners form sentences with names of other cities and countries.
4. Common nouns: Introduce common nouns in context. Have learners read simple sentences with common nouns from the board.
5. Put learners into groups to identify the common nouns. Have learners identify common nouns from paragraphs/passages.
6. Have learners form sentences with the common nouns identified.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.5.3.1: Apply knowledge of different types of nouns in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use collective nouns to refer to a group of objects and people
examplars:
1. Have learners read sentences containing collective nouns e.g. a team of players, a school of fish, a swarm of bees, a troop of monkeys, a flock of sheep, a bunch of keys.
2. Explain to learners that collective nouns behave like singular nouns. Therefore, they are used with singular verbs.
3. Provide collective nouns and their groups for learners to identify. Let them form sentences using the collective nouns.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.5.3.1: Apply knowledge of different types of nouns in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use abstract nouns to refer to concepts and ideas
examplars:
1. Revise common and proper nouns.
2. Write sentences containing abstract nouns on the board.
3. Have learners take turns to read them and identify the nouns.
4. Explain abstract nouns as names of things that cannot be seen, felt, nor touched. e.g. air, beauty, peace.
5. Have learners give examples of abstract nouns and write them on the board.
6. Have learners identify concrete and abstract nouns from paragraphs/passages. Let them use these abstract nouns in their own sentences.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.5.4.1: Demonstrate understanding of verbs in everyday language
indicator_description: Use the singular and plural subjects and the verb forms that go with them
examplars:
1. Revise the simple present form of verbs and their use to agree with the subject they follow.
2. Have learners do an activity: e. g. Write a letter to your friend telling him/her what you and your siblings do after school.
3. Learners read their friendly letters in pairs.
4. Guide learners to correct the wrong use of subjects and their appropriate verb forms that go with them if any. e.g. we eats -> we eat.
5. Have learners do peer editing to identify and correct errors.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.5.4.1: Demonstrate understanding of verbs in everyday language
indicator_description: Demonstrate the use of the simple past form in speech and in writing to express past conditions
examplars:
1. Revise the simple past tense (both regular and irregular verbs).
2. Assign learners to do an activity e.g. let learners, in small groups, re-write the letters they wrote previously in the simple past tense (Refer to B4.5.4.1.1).
3. Let pairs of learners exchange their work and peer edit.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.5.4.1: Demonstrate understanding of verbs in everyday language
indicator_description: Use the present perfect form of verbs to relate past events to the present
examplars:
1. Learners in pairs plan what they will do during the next weekend.
2. They put their plan into writing using modal auxiliaries.
3. Let two pairs come together to form bigger groups to edit their work one after the other.
4. Pairs of learners read their work to the class for discussion.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.5.5.1: Demonstrate understanding of adjectives in speech and in writing
indicator_description: Use adjectives to make comparisons e.g. fast /slow, good/bad, fast/faster, slow/slower
examplars:
1. Guide learners with several examples and situations to identify the comparative forms of adjectives in sentences, e.g. Ama is fast but Kofi is slow.
2. Have learners identify adjectives in a given passage.
3. Let learners write sentences using comparative and superlative forms of adjectives.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.5.5.1: Demonstrate understanding of adjectives in speech and in writing
indicator_description: Differentiate between how the comparative and superlative adjective forms are used in sentences
examplars:
1. Guide learners with examples to talk about their siblings using adjectives.
2. In pairs, learners write two to three sentences to describe their classmates using the three forms of adjectives.
3. Two pairs put their work together and do a comparison. Let them share their work with other groups.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.5.6.1: Understand and use adverbs correctly in speech and in writing
indicator_description: Use adverbs to talk about when and where the action of a verb took place
examplars:
1. Guided with examples, let learners narrate events using adverbs to describe the place and time of occurrence.
2. Introduce drills to provide oral practice.
3. Learners in group go on a field trip/excursion to e.g. a tourist centre.
4. Let them write and present their stories to demonstrate their knowledge of adverbs of place and time.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.5.7.1: Apply the knowledge of prepositions in oral and written communication
indicator_description: Use prepositions to convey a variety of meanings: Direction, e.g. up, Period of Time, e.g. for, Purpose, e.g. to, Possession, of, Comparison e.g. taller than
examplars:
1. Have learners describe the positions of classroom objects using such sentences.
2. Write sentences that are missing propositions on the board e.g. i. The boy is walking the road. ii. My auntie has stayed with us one week. iii. I am happy see you. iv. Show me a picture your pet. v. Kwaku is taller Ama.
3. Have learners tell what is wrong with the sentences. (They don't make sense. There are missing words, etc.) Write examples of the target prepositions on the board: up, for, to, of, than.
4. Put learners into groups and have them re-write the sentences correctly, using the given prepositions. Groups write their answers on the board and underline the words.
5. Guide learners to discover the functions of these prepositions.
6. Have learners work in pairs to use the prepositions in sentences. Direction = towards the mountain. Time = at six o'clock. Purpose = for, in order to. Possession = for, to. Comparison = than. Support or opposition = for you, against me.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.5.8.1: Apply the knowledge of conjunctions in speech and in writing
indicator_description: Identify and use conjunctions - and, but, or, nor-to link: similar ideas, contrasting ideas, express alternatives
examplars:
1. Note: Learners are to consolidate the knowledge of conjunctions acquired in Strand 3: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels.
2. Guide learners to give examples of sentences demonstrating their knowledge of conjunctions. e.g. i. Musa and Kuma are good friends. ii. Kwame is hard working but Abena is lazy.
3. Guide learners in groups to join sentences using coordinating conjunctions on a topic. e. g. A visit to an interesting tourist site.
4. Guide them to edit each other's work by exchanging with other groups.
5. Let learners talk about the differences in their stories.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Using Simple, Compound and Complex Sentences
content_standard: B4.5.9.1: Apply knowledge of grammatical rules to form words, phrases and sentences
indicator_description: Identify subjects and verbs in simple sentences
examplars:
1. Give learners an extract from a comprehension passage.
2. Guide them to identify the simple sentences. Let them identify subjects and verbs in each sentence.
3. Let learners write an event they have participated in.
4. Learners edit their work to demonstrate their knowledge of subject and predicate.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Using Simple, Compound and Complex Sentences
content_standard: B4.5.9.1: Apply knowledge of grammatical rules to form words, phrases and sentences
indicator_description: Construct simple sentences correctly
examplars:
1. Learners construct simple sentences.
2. Write some of the sentences on the board to guide the discussions on subjects and verbs.
3. Show a large poster of people doing various activities.
4. Assign learners to build a paragraph on the picture paying attention to subject and predicate. They use a line to divide each sentence into the subject and predicate.
5. The groups edit and publish the work. Each member keeps a copy.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B4.5.10.1: Spell words accurately
indicator_description: Use invented spelling to increase fluency and free writing
examplars:
1. Let learners spell given words with invented spellings. Each group tries to write the correct spelling. The group to get the highest number of words spelt correctly is regarded as the Spelling Champion for the work.
2. Give learners the meanings of words to identify and spell the words.
3. Learners use the words they have spelt in sentences of their own.
4. Select registers of various subjects/topics (shopping, transportation, agriculture).
5. Guide learners to spell the appropriate vocabulary used in that context.
6. Let learners use the words in writing a short paragraph describing or narrating that activity (shopping, travelling by bus, making a farm, catching fish).

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Building the Love and Culture of Reading
content_standard: B4.6.1.1: Read widely for pleasure, personal development, and demonstrate independent reading and learning in the literary content areas
indicator_description: Read a variety of age- and level appropriate books and present a-two-paragraph summary of each book read
examplars:
1. Guide learners to choose and read independently books of their choice during the library period.
2. Learners think-pair-share their stories with peers.
3. Ask each learner to write a-two-paragraph summary of the book read.
4. Invite individuals to present their work to the class for feedback.
5. Encourage them to visit the local library to read and borrow books.
6. Encourage them to share whatever they read with their mates.
